issue_description: status has casing/typo variants outside the canonical set: 'DELIVERED','Delivered','delivrd'.
metric_bias: Variants split 'delivered' into 4 buckets -> delivery_rate, revenue and on-time % all under-count.

issue_description: accepted_ts is later than picked_up_ts (impossible ordering).
metric_bias: Negative/garbage prep duration biases avg prep-time accuracy.
proposed_fix: Flag has_temporal_issue; exclude from prep/delivery timing metrics.

issue_description: picked_up_ts is later than delivered_ts (impossible ordering).
metric_bias: Negative pickup->delivery minutes inflate/!corrupt courier speed metrics.
proposed_fix: Flag has_temporal_issue; exclude from delivery-timing metrics.

issue_description: delivered_ts is populated although the order is not in a delivered status.
metric_bias: Phantom deliveries would over-count completed deliveries if delivered_ts were trusted blindly.
proposed_fix: Trust normalized_status, not raw delivered_ts; flag the conflict.

issue_description: total differs from subtotal - discount beyond a 0.01 tolerance (penny rounding drift).
metric_bias: Tiny per-order revenue drift; negligible in aggregate but visible in reconciliation.
proposed_fix: Keep raw values; flag has_amount_drift; reconcile to payments, not arithmetic.

issue_description: sum of successful payments for an order does not match the order total (tolerance 0.01).
metric_bias: GMV vs collected-cash mismatch; refund/partial-capture leakage if ignored.
proposed_fix: Flag has_payment_mismatch; report cash-vs-GMV gap separately.

issue_description: a customer rating exists for an order that was never delivered.
metric_bias: Ratings on cancelled/rejected orders would skew restaurant rating averages.
proposed_fix: Only count ratings for delivered orders in rating metrics.

issue_description: promo_codes.campaign_id references a campaign_id absent from marketing_campaigns.
metric_bias: Orphan promos cannot be attributed to a campaign -> campaign ROI is understated.
proposed_fix: Flag orphan promos; exclude from campaign roll-ups; chase source fix upstream.

issue_description: current_uses exceeds max_uses (a redemption cap was breached or counter is wrong).
metric_bias: Over-redeemed promos mean unbudgeted discount spend.
proposed_fix: Flag for finance review; do not auto-cap historical facts.

issue_description: the static current_uses counter disagrees with redemptions actually observed in orders.
metric_bias: current_uses is a snapshot; trusting it over observed orders mis-states redemption rate.
proposed_fix: Prefer observed redemptions from fact_orders_clean over the snapshot counter.

issue_description: order.city differs from the restaurant's registered city.
metric_bias: Could be a legitimate cross-border delivery, a multi-city brand, or a data error. Auto-rewriting city would corrupt geo metrics either way.
proposed_fix: Surface has_city_mismatch=true and let ops decide the source of truth per case.

issue_description: an order used a promo whose placed_ts falls outside [valid_from, valid_to] (date-only window, +1d grace).
metric_bias: Either promos were honored past expiry (revenue leakage) OR validity dates are stored date-only and the business allows same-day grace. The rule is genuinely ambiguous.
proposed_fix: Quantify and escalate to Marketing to confirm the real validity rule before enforcing.

issue_description: the scenario references customer-support tickets but no support_tickets.csv is provided.
metric_bias: Real ticket-rate / complaint-category metrics are impossible; only proxies can be computed.
proposed_fix: Document the gap; compute refund/cancellation/payment-issue PROXIES and label them clearly.

issue_description: app_events has no campaign_id or impression events, so ad exposure is unobservable.
metric_bias: Campaign impressions and true conversion (impression->order) cannot be computed.
proposed_fix: Set impressions = NULL; report redemptions / revenue / cost-per-redeemed-order instead.
